sections.title: A: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: A defining (1) ...... (CHARACTER) of the digital age is that individuals are constantly exposed to information from every corner of the globe.

Whereas previous generations could live (2) ...... (SEPARATE) from global events, widespread internet access now means that developments in distant countries feel anything but (3) ...... (FAMILIAR) to us.
When news spreads without context, (4) ...... (RACE) and other forms of prejudice can quickly take hold online.
Many users feel a deep (5) ...... (LOYAL) to particular platforms or online communities and struggle to develop an (6) ...... (APPRECIATE) of perspectives that differ from their own.

There is growing concern that our digital (7) ...... (INHERIT) — the data we leave behind — is at risk, and that careless practices could lead to the (8) ...... (APPEAR) of privacy as we once knew it.

Whether such fears are grounded in evidence or are merely a matter of (9) ...... (PERCEIVE) remains debated, yet most experts agree that living in (10) ...... (RELATE) safety online requires education and awareness.

sections.title: B: Gerund or Infinitive
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: We've agreed to **meet / meeting** the project manager at the office first thing tomorrow.
Would you mind to **lower / lowering** your voice? I'm trying to concentrate.
No circumstance justifies to **speak / speaking** to a colleague in that manner.
The coach was unhappy about the sponsor giving the team such expensive equipment, so they were made **return / to return** it.
I'll never forget to **watch / watching** the sun rise over the mountains on our first morning there.
Please don't hesitate to **reach / reaching** out to the support team should you need any assistance.
At this rate, we're going to end up to **lose / losing** our reservation if we don't leave soon.
I didn't dare to **bring / bringing** up the subject of the cancelled holiday with my sister.

sections.title: C: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
Elena and her grandmother are remarkably similar in character. **after**
Elena really ...... , doesn't she?

I noticed I was sitting in the wrong lecture hall entirely. **myself**
I ...... in completely the wrong lecture hall.

The first question in the test was so simple that nobody expected anyone to get it wrong. **play**
The first question ...... , so nobody expected anyone to get it wrong.

Daniel and his new flatmate have absolutely nothing they both enjoy. **common**
Daniel doesn't ...... his new flatmate.

Once she finishes her PhD, she'll be able to pursue her passion for painting. **free**
Completing her PhD ...... her passion for painting.
items.answer: will free her up to pursue

items.sentence:
My new neighbour and I got along brilliantly from the very first day. **house**
My new neighbour and I ...... from the very first day.
items.answer: got on like a house on fire

items.sentence:
Are you in the mood for a walk along the seafront this afternoon? **like**
Do you ...... along the seafront this afternoon?

Tom resigned because he and the head of department had very different views. **eye**
Tom resigned because he ...... the head of department.
items.answer: didn't see eye to eye with

sections.title: D: Prepositions and Fixed Phrases
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Sofia was fortunate to be born .................... a family with a long tradition of academic achievement.
The old lighthouse features .................... a short film about the region's maritime history.
Many economists argue that the minimum wage should be equal .................... at least sixty percent of the median salary.
The proposed regulation is problematic .................... one key respect: it fails to specify who is responsible for enforcement.
The committee decided it would not be .................... the public interest to release the full report at this stage.
This particular variety of orchid is native .................... the rainforests of Borneo.
The director was unable to attend the awards ceremony .................... person, so a colleague accepted the prize on her behalf.
Sailing across the bay at dawn is, for many visitors, the nearest thing .................... a truly peaceful experience.
Her latest statement ………………… everything she claimed in the previous interview.
The lecturer paused ………………… the diagram on the board before moving on.
Research suggests that the middle ………………… in a family often develops strong negotiating skills.
Critics have long considered Herrera ………………… one of the most innovative architects of her era.
The delay was eventually attributed to a serious administrative …………………… .
Experts are questioning whether the digital ………………… between urban and rural areas is narrowing.
Curious onlookers began to ………………… around the street performer as soon as the music started.
The intern didn't deserve ………………… criticised so harshly in front of the entire team.
